Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails in Samira's inbox for the portal data exposure escalation. Use search_emails(query: "portal" or "Terraform" or "data exposure" or similar). Agent finds email_mina_samira_portal_escalation_001 (Mina Hashimoto to Samira Tariq, April 25 08:30 UTC, CC: david.chen@moveops.com): Mina forwarding Rachel Nguyen's complaint that Terraform admin users can see cross-client relocation records in the portal. Agent confirms David Chen is CC'd on the escalation.
OE 2: Search emails for Rachel's follow-up message containing the CISO's formal incident report requirements. Use search_emails(query: "CISO" or "incident report" or "follow-up" or "portal" or similar). Agent finds email_rachel_mina_portal_followup_001 (Rachel Nguyen to Mina Hashimoto, April 25 16:00 UTC, unread): the CISO has formally requested an incident report by end of day Monday April 27 answering four specific questions - (1) what data was exposed and to whom, (2) how many client company records were accessible through the portal, (3) how long the exposure window has been open, (4) what remediation steps are being taken.
OE 3: Search for additional Rachel-to-Mina follow-ups to get the full picture of the CISO's requirements. Use search_emails(query: "Rachel" or "CISO" or "portal" or similar). Agent finds email_rachel_mina_portal_export_update_001 (Rachel Nguyen to Mina Hashimoto, April 25 19:00 UTC, unread, replying to the CISO follow-up): Derek Liu ran a bulk CSV export from the admin portal on approximately April 15 for quarterly headcount reporting. The file is on his local machine and likely contains the full unfiltered dataset across all MoveOps clients. The CISO is adding a fifth question to the incident report request: were any bulk data exports or downloads performed through the portal during the exposure window, and if so, on what dates and by which admin users.
OE 4: Search Slack for the engineering root cause discussion about the portal incident. Use conversations_search_messages(search_query: "portal" or "tenant" or "data isolation" or similar) or conversations_history(channel_id: "C003"). Agent finds Anh Nguyen's message in #engineering (C003, ts: 1777125600.000000, April 25 14:00 UTC): the admin dashboard list view and bulk export endpoint queries Airtable tblRelocations01 without a tenant_id parameter, returning all client relocation records to any portal admin user. Anh offers to patch the API query layer with a company_id filter.
OE 5: Retrieve thread replies on Anh's Slack message. Use conversations_replies(channel_id: "C003", thread_ts: "1777125600.000000"). Agent finds Dmitri Volkov's reply (ts: 1777127400.000000, April 25 14:30 UTC): he flagged this exact gap during the portal sprint in March, left a comment on the portal setup ticket warning the API returns all records without tenant filtering, was marked P2 at the time because Terraform was the only portal client.
OE 6: Search Linear for the portal setup ticket Dmitri referenced in the Slack thread. Use linear_list_issues(team: "team_engineering") or a search with query related to "portal" or "Terraform" or "Vantage" or "admin" (or similar). Agent finds issue_portal_admin_setup (ENG-370, "Self-serve portal admin configuration - Vantage Distributed", state: done, labels: ["portal", "terraform", "onboarding"], assignee: Anh Nguyen, created March 15 2026, completed March 21 2026).
OE 7: Get comments on issue_portal_admin_setup to find Dmitri's original warning. Use linear_list_comments(issueId: "issue_portal_admin_setup") or retrieve the full issue. Agent finds comment_dmitri_tenant_isolation_warning (Dmitri Volkov, March 25 2026): detailed warning that the admin API returns all relocation records across all MoveOps clients without filtering by company, recommends adding a company_id filter before GA. Marked P2 per Samira since it would be addressed before next enterprise portal onboarding.
OE 8: Search emails to find the message Julian received about the portal issue. Use search_emails(query: "Julian" or "Derek" or "portal" or "unfamiliar" or similar). Agent finds email_derek_julian_portal_data_001 (Derek Liu to julian.brooks@moveops.com, April 24 16:00 UTC): Derek independently noticed unfamiliar employee names (Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, Keiko Tanaka) when pulling a portal report, asked Julian whether this was test data or records from other companies. This is a second independent confirmation of the exposure from a Terraform admin, sent 16 hours before Rachel's formal report.
OE 9: Search CRM and QuickBooks for the Terraform Digital account to retrieve financial and incident data. Use crm_search_companies(name: "Terraform Digital") or crm_list_engagements(company_ids: ["company-terraform-digital"]) or similar. Agent finds engagement_terraform_data_exposure_apr25: Terraform enterprise contract valued at $1,850,000, this is the second data handling incident in under 6 weeks, contract risk elevated. CRM confirms exposed data includes records from at least two other MoveOps clients - Vectral Systems and Mosaic Robotics. Agent also checks QuickBooks via quickbooks_list_invoices or quickbooks_get_invoice and finds invoice NMD-2026-0042 for $462,500 with due date April 17 and full balance outstanding - meaning the invoice is past due as of April 25.
OE 9b: Query Airtable to verify the full scope of relocation records exposed through the portal. Use airtable_list_records(table_id: "tblRelocations01") or similar. Agent retrieves all records from tblRelocations01 and confirms that relocation records for exactly 14 unique client companies are present in the table - establishing that any Terraform portal admin using the unfiltered endpoint would have had access to data from all 14 companies, not just the two named in the CRM engagement.
OE 10: Look up email addresses for incident email recipients and meeting attendees. Use contacts_search_contacts(query: "Mina") -> mina.hashimoto@moveops.com; contacts_search_contacts(query: "David" or "David Chen") -> david.chen@moveops.com; contacts_search_contacts(query: "Anh") -> anh.nguyen@moveops.com; contacts_search_contacts(query: "Dmitri") -> dmitri.volkov@moveops.com.
OE 11: Create a P0 Linear incident ticket for the portal data isolation failure. Use linear_create_issue(team: "team_engineering", title: referencing portal data isolation or cross-client data exposure or similar, priority: 1, labels: ["portal", "terraform"], assignee: "moveops_anh_nguyen" (Anh Nguyen, who identified the root cause in OE 4), description: documenting root cause - missing tenant_id/company_id filter in Airtable API query layer - and referencing or linking to issue_portal_admin_setup ENG-370).
OE 12: Send an incident summary email from Samira to Mina and David. Use send_email(sender: "samira.tariq@moveops.com", recipients: ["mina.hashimoto@moveops.com", "david.chen@moveops.com"], content: addressing all five CISO questions from OE 2 and OE 3 - (1) which employee records were exposed including specific names from Rachel's report, (2) that records from 14 client companies were accessible through the portal per Airtable verification, (3) the exposure timeline tracing back to March 2026 per Dmitri's warning, (4) Anh's remediation plan to add a company_id filter to the API query layer, and (5) whether any bulk data exports were performed during the exposure window including Derek Liu's CSV export on approximately April 15 - written so Mina can forward it directly to Rachel's CISO without edits).
OE 13: Create a calendar event for Monday April 27 2026 morning. Use calendar_add_calendar_event(title: "Portal Data Isolation Incident Review" or similar, start_datetime: Monday April 27 2026 morning, attendees: samira.tariq@moveops.com, anh.nguyen@moveops.com [OE 4 root cause discoverer], dmitri.volkov@moveops.com [OE 5 and OE 7 original warner], david.chen@moveops.com [OE 1 escalation notification recipient]).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

outcome

✓Run #1The agent called `linear_create_issue` and it returned success with id 'issue_5d9b8029e32843f58e7d4ca8479b420e'. The title is 'P0: Admin portal serving cross-client relocation data — tenant isolation failure', which contains both 'portal' and references to isolation/exposure.
✓Run #2The agent called linear_create_issue and the tool returned success with issue id 'issue_0dbc8e6b6e8343719b1cb5ad059b8353', number 483. The title is 'P0: Cross-tenant data exposure in admin portal — no company_id filtering on relocation API', which clearly references 'portal' and 'data exposure'.
✓Run #3linear_create_issue was called with title 'P0: Admin portal multi-tenant data isolation failure — cross-client relocation records exposed to Terraform Digital'. The tool returned a successful result with issue id 'issue_373debbd401441bc989ab1eb4cf5f1bf' and number 483.
✓Run #4linear_create_issue was called and returned success with id 'issue_e0b969d546614473be4b1643a7ce771c'. Title: 'P0: Portal tenant isolation failure — cross-client relocation data exposed to Terraform admins' — contains 'portal' and 'data exposed' equivalent.
✓Run #5The agent called linear_create_issue and the tool returned success (issue ID issue_90efb85279fe4a7fa54524b6edc497fb, number 483). The title is 'P0: Cross-client data exposure in admin portal — missing tenant isolation', which contains 'portal' and 'data exposure'.
✓Run #6linear_create_issue was called with title 'P0: Admin portal missing tenant isolation — cross-client relocation data exposed to Terraform Digital' and returned success with issue ID 'issue_d6791269e8af4974b2b68bd652c9f38f' (ENG-483). The title references 'portal' and 'data exposed' (equivalent to data exposure).
2 The P0 Linear issue description documents the root cause as the missing tenant or company filter on the portal API's Airtable query - for example: no tenant_id parameter, unfiltered relocation records returned to all portal admins, or equivalent based on Anh's Slack analysis.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The Linear issue description explicitly states: 'without any tenant/company_id filter' and in the Root Cause section: 'The admin dashboard list view and export endpoints query the Airtable relocations table directly without passing a tenant_id / company_id parameter scoped to the authenticated admin user's company association.' This directly documents the missing tenant filter as root cause.
✓Run #2The Linear issue description explicitly states: 'The admin portal dashboard and bulk export endpoints pull relocation records directly from the Airtable tblRelocations01 table via our internal API without any tenant/company_id filtering.' and 'The admin API query layer does not pass a tenant_id / company_id parameter when fetching records from the Airtable relocations table.' The root cause is clearly documented.
✓Run #3The Linear issue description explicitly states: 'query the Airtable relocations table (tblRelocations01) via the internal API without passing a tenant_id / company_id filter. The authenticated admin user's company association is available in the SSO token but is never used to scope the query.' Root cause is clearly documented.
✓Run #4The Linear issue description explicitly states: 'query tblRelocations01 via the internal API without passing a tenant_id or company_id filter scoped to the authenticated admin user's company' — clearly documents the missing tenant/company filter as root cause.
✓Run #5The Linear issue description explicitly states: 'The admin portal's relocation dashboard and bulk export endpoint pull records directly from the Airtable relocations table (tblRelocations01) via our internal API without any tenant/company filter' and 'The admin API query layer does not pass a tenant_id or company_id parameter when fetching relocation records.' This clearly documents the missing tenant filter root cause.
✓Run #6The Linear issue description explicitly states: 'The admin dashboard API endpoint and bulk export endpoint pull records directly from Airtable (tblRelocations01) without passing a tenant_id or company_id filter parameter. Admin users are authenticated via SSO tokens with company association, but the query layer does not use this association to scope results.' Root cause is clearly documented.
3 The P0 Linear issue is assigned to Anh Nguyen (assignee contains 'Anh' or '[anh.nguyen@moveops.com](mailto:anh.nguyen@moveops.com)' or 'moveops_anh_nguyen' or similar).
outcome

✓Run #2The linear_create_issue call included 'assignee': 'moveops_anh_nguyen', and the returned result confirms assignee_id is 'moveops_anh_nguyen'.
✓Run #3The linear_create_issue call includes 'assignee': 'Anh Nguyen'. The final response also states 'ENG-483, assigned to Anh Nguyen'.
✓Run #4The linear_create_issue call passed assignee='Anh' and the returned JSON confirms assignee_id='moveops_anh_nguyen', correctly assigned to Anh Nguyen.
✓Run #5The linear_create_issue call used assignee 'Anh Nguyễn' and the returned issue has assignee_id 'moveops_anh_nguyen', confirming the issue was assigned to Anh Nguyen.
✓Run #6The linear_create_issue call used assignee 'Anh Nguyễn', and the returned JSON shows assignee_id 'moveops_anh_nguyen' which was confirmed via linear_list_users as anh.nguyen@moveops.com.

9 The incident email body includes remediation steps referencing adding a tenant or company filter to the portal API query, or Anh patching the query layer with a company_id filter, or equivalent concrete technical fix (or similar).
outcome

✓Run #1The incident email states: 'Anh Nguyen has identified the root cause — the API query layer has no tenant_id/company_id parameter. She is ready to patch the query layer to add company-scoped filtering to all list, detail, and export endpoints.' This directly references the concrete technical fix.
✓Run #2The email body specifically states: 'Adding company_id filtering to the admin API query layer for both the relocation list view and the bulk CSV export endpoint. This is the direct fix and is being deployed by Anh Nguyen under P0 priority (ENG-483).' The concrete technical fix is clearly referenced.
✓Run #3The email states: 'Anh Nguyen is patching the API query layer to add company_id filtering based on the authenticated admin user's company association. This covers both the list view and the export endpoint.'
✓Run #4The email explicitly states: 'Adding company_id filtering to the admin API query layer so both the dashboard list view and the bulk CSV export endpoint return only records belonging to the authenticated admin's company' — concrete technical fix referencing company_id filter.
✓Run #5The email body states: 'Anh Nguyen is patching the admin API query layer to add company_id filtering. The portal authenticates admin users via SSO tokens that include a company association — this association exists but was not being used to scope the data query.' This is a concrete technical fix with the company_id filter referenced.
✓Run #6The email explicitly states: 'Anh Nguyễn is patching the portal query layer to filter all API responses — both the dashboard list view and the bulk export endpoint — by the company_id associated with the authenticated admin user's SSO token. This is the root cause: the query pulls from our Airtable relocations table without passing a tenant filter.'

14 The final response identifies the root cause as the portal admin API not applying a company or tenant filter when querying Airtable relocation records - all records returned to any portal admin without scoping to that client's employees.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response states: 'The admin portal API queries the Airtable relocations table (tblRelocations01) with no tenant/company_id filter. Every record across all 14 MoveOps clients is returned to any portal admin — both in the dashboard list view and the bulk CSV export.' This clearly identifies the root cause.
✓Run #2The final response states: 'The admin portal's relocation dashboard and CSV export endpoints pull data from Airtable (tblRelocations01) without any company_id filtering. Any Terraform admin user could see every relocation record across all MoveOps clients — not just their own.' The root cause (missing company_id filter) is clearly identified.
✓Run #3The final response states: 'The admin portal API queries tblRelocations01 without any tenant/company filter. Every authenticated portal admin sees every client's relocation records.' The root cause as missing tenant/company filter is clearly identified.
✓Run #4The final response states: 'The admin portal API queries tblRelocations01 without any company_id filter. Every portal admin — regardless of which company they belong to — sees the full dataset. Both the dashboard list view and the bulk CSV export are affected.' — correct technical root cause stated.
✓Run #5The final response states: 'The admin portal API pulls relocation records from Airtable (tblRelocations01) without any tenant/company filter. The SSO tokens correctly associate admin users with their company, but that association was never wired into the data query layer.' This clearly identifies the root cause.
✓Run #6The final response states: 'The admin portal's API and bulk export endpoint pull from tblRelocations01 without any tenant/company filter.' Root cause clearly identified as missing tenant/company filter on the portal API's Airtable query.

23 The model must pass a query related to 'portal' or 'tenant' or 'data isolation' or 'Anh' (or similar) when calling conversations_search_messages to find the engineering root cause discussion in Slack.

25 The model must use an Airtable read tool (e.g., airtable_list_records, airtable_search_records, or similar) to verify the full scope from source data.
tool selection
